title: The Rise of Spock Brow
excerpt: Hello everyone I have been I'm sorry I haven't been around very much recently doing videos I'm up to my neck in e-learning I'm producing a convocations course which is going to cover everything in Botox and dermal fillers is a ton of work it's really a lot to cover but I'm enjoyi…

segments.text: hello everyone I have been I'm sorry I haven't been around very much recently doing videos I'm up to my neck in e-learning I'm producing a convocations course which is going to cover everything in Botox and dermal fillers is a ton of work it's really a lot to cover but I'm enjoying it learning a lot and I will share it with you shortly but I've been pulled out of the darkness nearly to do a little video on brow ptosis because and sorry Spock brows what was pulled me into the light again and it's because there's a little video going around which has confused a few people it's basically a clinician who's saying that we should always inject over here to avoid a brow better Spock brow and I'm here to share my point of view because I think that's a there's a recipe for a lot of cross sad ladies in their 40s and 50s and our

most common aging aesthetics for anti-aging treatments is 43 so if you're treating 25 year olds this may not apply if you're treating 43 year olds like most of us you must be careful of injecting in this area and I'm gonna explain to you why and just go over some of the concepts and that you need to know before treating the frontalis muscle so first thing frontalis is the hardest area to treat absolutely is and the reason for that is because of exactly why we're having this conversation which is you may cause a brow job but you also can cause too much lift and it can look at unpleasant for different reasons so and we've got to try and find a balance and that's the key word balance how do you find a balance and what is your route to finding that balance that gives you the most options as you progress along adjusting someone's muscle because everyone's unique every forehead is slightly different so we need to leave the door open to make adjustments you know in a way that is most likely to get us to the best place possible so that's why I train in the way that I have so let's go and have a look at that I've got a oh look at those eyebrows I'd

meant to have them off I was going to reveal them later there we go I'll take them off for a bit so um this is these are the muscles obviously we're treating the what I want to basically make really is that this is a conceptual like and I think a lot of people and they like me to draw dots on things as soon as possible but the reality is you need to understand the concept so the main concept that we're talking about is about the vectors across the face or the forces that decide where a muscle and where an eyebrow is so they're essentially three muscles that are affecting your eyebrow you can have there's one that pulls your eyebrows medially the corrugator there's a muscle group to lift them up your frontalis and of course there's a muscle that pulls them down which is your z line pull down that's your orbicularis oculi so your muscle is in a state your eyebrows in a state of equilibrium that is caused by the balance of these forces if you change these forces you can change the position of your eyebrow and

the fourth force is gravity which is more and more important as you get older and that's why this video is a little bit tricky if you start applying it to all of your patients so let's go back to this and the concept the concept that I tend to try and treat teach is that this is we need to think about areas of muscle relax rather than injection points I know everyone loves to see injection points because it saves you from thinking but unfortunately where there's no one there to think for you and all you're thinking about is injection points you can make a mistake particularly with the frontalis so if the green area is the area that the toxin has relaxed we need to think about this green area and make sure that the green area corresponds to that the result that we want to create so first thing is what would cause a Spock brow if you have a look back at and Spock you can see he's got loads of lateral lift and then medially it looks like a drop so heavy in the middle

lifted on the lateral side and the pattern that would produce this would be very little treatment laterally so that sort of shape and too much treatment medially so we're knocking out the effect the supporting role of the central frontalis and we are and not treating in enough with enough toxin the lateral brow so what do you get as so that's what a Spock brow is remember that that's an important point that there are two components to it there's too much lift laterally and too little support immediately that's what makes us Bach brow if I was to do that you wouldn't have a Spock brow you would just have too much movement you'd have a line that ran all the way across here and similarly if I was to do that bit but also treat this you also wouldn't have a Spock brow because you'd have no movement here and that's actually what this video that is suggesting that we do is that you treat over here to prevent this lifting and then to compensate for the fact that it might lift and you know sorry that it might be a bit heavy there's also a

suggestion that you we should put toxin in here as well and which is obviously a way of lifting but it's going to be that that's a little bit of a riskier injection to inject underneath the eyebrow let me put my brows on so you can see it and there we go so if we're injecting here underneath the eyebrow that will definitely enable your that will prove reduce the chance of this pattern of injecting causing a drop but unfortunately it's leaving you with very little options that follow up if you have got a drop because this has been a bit strong so isn't there a better way than relying on that injection to prevent a drop yes there is and you need to leave some space here so that you can use that as a back-up plan so we leave a bit more space here and then we have a back-up plan because you can still get heaviness trust me if you treat this area even if you leave a bit of space sometimes it feels heavy anyway and then at least you've got this back-up plan and be able to put some toxin here so that's what I mean about and we're creating a route like it's not about what should be

work if we just try once like it's a journey that you're going to find that you want to start particularly when you first start injecting knowing that you've got options if it doesn't work out the way you want so we try and do a little soft eye brow lift not too much of an eyebrow lift because we don't want Spock brows and then if it lifts too much you can add a bit more and you can soften it but also if it drops what do you do then well you can inject the orbicularis oculi and then you can lift it again so we're always trying to leave the door open for an adjustment later try and do basically try and get the result with the least amount of product don't try and get right at once using injections in all the places and then you've left the door closed for adjustments later which is what I think this video and that's popping around is basically doing as you Europe you're sealing off your options and you're not gonna be able to make the adjustment later so let me talk you through the way you design a treatment with respect to eyebrows because that also comes up a lot so the first thing to know is that you're a typical eyebrow and a beautiful

eyebrow should say should arch in a female on the point that runs between the Eila base and the mid peculiar line and that's where the arch should be so if you want an eyebrow to the little lift in a way that's consistent with that arch you'd also want it to lift and along this vector so this force that we create that is and that's how you'd where you'd want the eyebrows to lift this is another thing that can cause spot browsers if you lift too laterally over here and that that's the thing that's most Dutch shape is most likely to alter the shape of your eyebrow so you're gonna be lifting one of this will work and there we go so you're lifting like that that's a spot brow right so this is another thing is if you're if you create a lift but you created two lateral that's gonna cause a SPOC brow that wasn't really particularly made much of in the video but it's to do with how much front arles that you're actually lifting and

where it's being lifted so we don't lift it there we want to lift it laterally if we want to lift it laterally and sorry we don't we want to lift it over the arch this is how you decide how to do is you draw this line from the Eila base through the arch up into the top of the forehead and that gives you the target now most people you don't want muscle particularly in this case this muscles quite long because therefore has relatively on the average the large side but if you leave all of this untreated all the way along this vector you'll get ladders that run right to the top of the forehead and it may lift too much so how do we soften that in a strategic way that doesn't cause other problems well here's what we do we take the distance between the tail of the arch and the point we want to lift and we use an equidistant point here and then linked up these three points so that now you have an area of muscle that tapers off instead of going up creating a kind of consistent strength like that that's what laddering does it tapers off so it's gonna be strongest down here and weakest up here because remember we're

gonna toxic what toxin in these other places so that's gonna soften that ability and but we want now so that will leave this muscle pulling the eyebrow so that will give you a good eyebrow lift now you don't always want an eyebrow lift and some people their eyebrows lift too easily as they get older because we've lost fat and some people and you just don't want too much of a lift so how would you soften it while you start by softening it by injecting in the top third this is assuming we still want to lift if you don't want to lift you don't need to draw this triangle and you can block off the whole lot and just inject all of that and but we're not going to do that we just want a little bit of lifts and we put one unit just here so there's still a decent amount of muscle in this section that you can actually lift the eyebrows with and so that that's the concept with an eyebrow lift and of course if you drop them already then you can inject underneath the eyebrow like suggest about it but it's the wrong way around we need to first create a lift if it's too strong soften it and then if you cause a drop you can undo it and lift it and but

that's the main that's how you decide where not to inject in order to cause a lateral brow lift and not too much for lift so how do we prevent a medial brow ptosis because that's the other problem that we have and that is to do with and drawing also on these little lines which I usually start with the middle line so if you draw up from the mid pupilary line cross the orbital rim which is about here in the eyebrow and keep going for about two centimeters that this then creates and you're top of your board and you should then draw a line across to the other side so the to match and then this area underneath here is where you don't inject so this means you've got active muscle here and that will enable you to get them support for that medial brow and while not and let me just show you what this looks like underneath so if I take this green marker which I started off with and raised them up this is where you're not going to inject now that's too strong so I'll bring

that down to there and that's the pattern of a big eyebrow lift now usually you might do this as well there may be something here so they don't have lines right at the top if they go that high so this is the area that you treat and the pink area you can still see is the untreated area but in most people a particularly large forward you're gonna narrow that down slightly because you don't want so much of a lift that would be too strong and then you may put one injection at that point so you end up with something more like this it's kind of an M shape and let me get rid of some of these other things you can see bit better and that's how I create the M shape I create that vector that shows you the direction of the eyebrow lift and I do this little safety area here and then underneath that you can treat the corrugator as usual but that's how you prevent a medial brow lift medial Brad ptosis and this is how you prevent a lateral brow ptosis and the two together should give you also and this is the crucial point I think I've already said it but choose a

strategy that gives you options as you go on because that's what I don't like about this approach of inject here and then case it drops put a little bit under there but you don't even know it's going to drop you really don't you've already done your defensive measure before you need to that's not a good strategy so why don't we put less here and treat as usual that might be great if it's not you can add a bit more and if that causes a drop you still got the space underneath the eyebrow where you can lift it up again and that way you'll get you're learning you'll get a happy client more likely all have options if it doesn't go right and you can correct it and so I hope that helps it's really late and I just thought I had to do that for I go to bed and I hope it's helped you and I hope it's helped some of those stopped some of those questions coming up if you found that helpful tag someone share it give me a thumbs up and comment ask me more questions I do really like comments I'm sorry I don't always respond to every single one but I do read them it makes me happy inside so thank you very much and if it's helpful
please let me know thanks and I will speak to you can see
